page: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32 virtual CPUs derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. This configuration yields a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are also notable, as evidenced by a strong score in the Passmark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory performance is robust, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%, though large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average range. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves strong text generation speeds on small LLM models, though prompt processing speeds on larger 70B models are weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the m6a.8xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for deployments that do not require high-speed local IOPS or hardware acceleration. This makes the instance a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring steady CPU and memory allocation without the premium of specialized hardware. Appropriate workloads include database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale text processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a dedicated 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 4.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong memory scores, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Large-model LLM prompt processing is a notable performance bottleneck. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, strong single-core performance, and robust DDR4 memory capabilities.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPUs, utilizing network-attached storage
